title: Tapping for the Unearned Money
subtitle: An EFT sequence for the guilt and unworthiness that arrives with an inheritance, a gift, or a windfall
excerpt: An EFT tapping sequence for the guilt and discomfort that arrives when money does — without being earned in the expected way. Taps through the belief that unearned money is somehow borrowed or at risk, and toward the capacity to hold it steadily.
sourceNotes: Written for homemade.education. EFT framework developed by Gary Craig (mid-1990s).

practice.whenToUse: Use when you've received money you didn't earn — an inheritance, a gift, an insurance payout, a windfall — and guilt or discomfort is running alongside it.

content.text: An EFT tapping sequence for the guilt that shows up when money arrives without the usual work attached to it. Inheritances, gifts, legal settlements, windfalls — the money is real, but something underneath makes it feel like it doesn't belong to you yet.
New to tapping? Read
how tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop
Tap the karate chop point on the side of your hand and say each statement once:
Even though this money arrived without me earning it in the usual way and I don't fully trust that I can keep it, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I feel guilty having this money when I didn't work for it, something in me is open to letting it settle.
Even though I'm waiting for someone to say I shouldn't have this, I am willing to consider that it is mine.
Tapping round
Tap each point seven times while saying the phrase:
Eyebrow: I didn't earn this in the usual way.
Side of Eye: I'm waiting for the guilt to make sense.
Under Eye: It doesn't feel fully mine yet.
Under Nose: I keep thinking I should give some of it away.
Chin: Or that I'll lose it if I relax about it.
Collarbone: The guilt is real even when the logic isn't.
Under Arm: I was taught that money only counts if you worked for it.
Top of Head: This money is here. I don't know yet what to do with the guilt.
Reframe
Eyebrow: Receiving is a different kind of work — not lesser.
Side of Eye: The guilt is old. The money is new.
Under Eye: It can be mine to keep.
Under Nose: I don't need to give it away to prove I deserved it.
Chin: I can hold this steadily and decide from a calm head.
Collarbone: Money that was given to me is still mine.
Under Arm: I am allowed to receive without a full explanation.
Top of Head: This is mine. I let it settle.
Tap each round three times, then move to the reframe and tap that three times.
Where this practice comes from
Written for homemade.education. The EFT framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique, in use since the mid-1990s.
